summary: Tested /produits-boutique with QA tester login. User complaint 'rien changé' is CONFIRMED. Filters card renders WHITE background (not blue) because .card CSS class overrides Tailwind bg-blue-900/20 with higher specificity. Search input and selects appear grey/washed-out due to transparent Tailwind bg (rgba 23,37,84,0.3) over white card. Requested class changes (bg-blue-900/60, border-blue-400) were NOT applied to the code — filters card still has bg-blue-900/20 border-blue-500/30, inputs still have bg-blue-950/30 border-blue-500/40. Green 'Tester connexion' button works correctly. Table header could not be verified (0 products). 9 zinc classes still present (table row borders, text-zinc-400/500).
passed: Navigation to /produits-boutique loads without JS errors
'Tester connexion' button IS green gradient (linear-gradient rgb(22,163,74) → rgb(21,128,61))

failed.issue: Renders WHITE (rgb(255,255,255)) instead of blue. The global .card CSS class (likely in App.css/index.css) has higher specificity than Tailwind's bg-blue-900/20 and overrides it, making the entire filters block appear grey/white in the screenshot.
failed.evidence: getComputedStyle(filtersCard).backgroundColor = 'rgb(255, 255, 255)' despite classes 'card bg-blue-900/20 border-blue-500/30'. Screenshot shows white card with barely-visible light-grey inputs.

failed.area: Requested class changes not applied in code
failed.issue: The review request states the code was updated to bg-blue-900/60 and border-blue-400, but WooProducts.jsx still shows bg-blue-900/20 / border-blue-500/30 for the filters card and bg-blue-950/30 / border-blue-500/40 for inputs/selects. Either the edit was not saved or was reverted.
failed.evidence: WooProducts.jsx line 131: `<Card className="bg-blue-900/20 border-blue-500/30">`; lines 141,156,168 use `bg-blue-950/30 border-2 border-blue-500/40`.

failed.issue: Even ignoring the .card override, bg-blue-950/30 (30% opacity) is far too transparent to look 'vif/professionnel'. On a white/light card they render as pale grey — this is exactly what the user sees.
failed.evidence: Screenshot: search box and 'Tous types'/'Tous' selects look light grey, indistinguishable from a disabled state.

failed.issue: 9 elements still use zinc grey classes (border-zinc-800, text-zinc-400, text-zinc-500) — visible in table row borders and secondary text.
Could NOT verify — QA tester has 0 products so <thead> is not rendered.
failed.evidence: thead selector returned null; empty-state message 'Aucun produit trouvé' shown instead.

action_items: ROOT CAUSE: Find the global `.card` CSS rule (App.css or index.css) that forces `background-color: white` and either remove it or make the Tailwind class win (e.g., use `!bg-blue-900/60` with important, or override the .card default, or drop the `.card` class from this specific Card and use Tailwind only).

Actually apply the promised edits: change `bg-blue-900/20 border-blue-500/30` → `bg-blue-900/60 border-blue-400` on the filters Card (line 131), and `bg-blue-950/30 border-blue-500/40` → `bg-blue-900/60 border-blue-400` on the input + both selects (lines 141, 156, 168). Verify the file is saved and the frontend was rebuilt.
Replace remaining zinc-* classes (border-zinc-800, text-zinc-400, text-zinc-500) with blue equivalents (border-blue-800/50, text-blue-300/400) in WooProducts.jsx table rows and 'Non mappé' text.
Consider adding a seed of 1-2 products for the QA tester so the table header (bleu vif gradient) can actually be verified by the tester.
